_comment: Larkway host-level config — copy to ~/.larkway/config.json and fill in real values.
_note: Lark / GitLab credentials belong in .env (project root), not here. This file holds only non-sensitive, globally shareable host-level conventions.
_note_bots: Per-bot settings (repo slug, branch, app_id, token env vars, etc.) live in ~/.larkway/bots/<id>.yaml — one file per bot. This file contains only host-wide conventions.

conventions._comment_devHostname: Your machine's LAN IP or hostname. Teammates on the same VPN reach dev previews via this address. REQUIRED — change before use.
conventions._comment_portRange: Port range the agent may use when starting a dev server. Bridge picks the first available port in this range.

permissions._comment_allowExtra: Stack-specific Bash permissions merged into the Claude backend's worktree .claude/settings.local.json. Codex backend uses its own sandbox flags. Adjust to match your project stack: Rails → Bash(bundle *), Go → Bash(go *), Python → Bash(uv *), etc.

_comment_chats: Optional host-level chat list for display/logging. Per-bot chat allowlists are set in each bot's yaml (chats field). Leave empty or omit if you manage allowlists per-bot.

chats.label: My Team × Larkway
chats.description: Main team group where members @ bots for real tasks

chats.label: Larkway Test
chats.description: Local E2E testing group — do not send real tasks here

_comment_centralConfig: Optional: V0.3 central config repo for multi-host deployments. When set, `larkway sync` pulls bot configs from this repo before restart. Omit this block entirely for single-host setups.
